messages.content: What limitation regarding reward hacking does the paper acknowledge?

Automated graders may be exploited by attackers to appear successful against the grader rather than truly exposing defender weaknesses, posing a risk that the attacker might target the grading system instead of the model itself.
Which LLM model achieved the highest attack success rate on real‑world defenses?
Claude 3.7 Sonnet achieved a 21% success rate, outperforming other models like GPT‑4o and o1 on that subset.
How many SPARQL queries are executed during anomalous subgraph construction?
Three SPARQL queries are used: one for direct connections between anomalous nodes, and two for their one‑hop neighbors.
What is the attack success rate (ASR) of BEAST on Vicuna-7B-v1.5 within one GPU minute?
BEAST achieves an ASR of 89% on Vicuna-7B-v1.5 when limited to one GPU minute.
What was the main limitation of BEYOND PHISH highlighted in the paper regarding evolving scam tactics?
Scammers can manipulate URLs to appear legitimate, allowing BEYOND PHISH to miss such deceptive sites.
What is the batch size used during inference with vLLM for red‑team experiments?
A batch size of 1 is employed during all inference runs using vLLM on NVIDIA A100‑80GB GPUs.
How does COLD-Attack handle position constraints in attacks?
It inserts an adversarial sequence y between the user query x and a separate prompt p, optimizing for fluency of x ⊕ y ⊕ p while ensuring the combined text triggers harmful outputs.
Which forbidden scenario shows the highest vulnerability to jailbreaking across all models?
Political Lobbying has the highest ASR, reaching 0.855 on average across the six evaluated models.
In the adaptive GCG attack, what strategy is employed to reduce the gradient norm of malicious queries?

Adaptive‑GCG optimizes the jailbreak suffix not only for the original query but also for perturbed versions (adding Gaussian noise), encouraging consistency and thereby lowering the gradient norm below detection thresholds.
How many hallucinated instances were found when evaluating open‑ended TruthfulQA questions?
24 out of 100 sampled open‑ended responses were identified as hallucinations with a Cohen’s kappa of 0.97 between annotators.
What is the purpose of the Sankey diagram in the training flow view?
The Sankey diagram visualizes flows from risk types to toxicity levels within an iteration, showing how many prompts of each type lead to each response safety level.
What is the impact on human rater precision and recall when they receive keyword explanations from an LLM in a real‑world pilot?
Human raters experienced a 9% absolute increase in recall and an 11% absolute increase in precision, with no significant change in review time.
How does removing the top safety ranks affect model utility compared to removing the least important safety ranks?
Removing top safety ranks drastically reduces utility, dropping average accuracy near 0.35, whereas removing the least important safety ranks has minimal effect on utility.
Which 11 open‑source LLMs were evaluated with the generation exploitation attack?
VICUNA (7B, 13B, 33B), MPT (7B, 30B), FALCON (7B, 40B), LLAMA2 (7B, 13B, 7B‑chat, 13B‑chat) were the models tested.
What type of neural architecture underlies DART’s diffusion model?
An encoder‑decoder transformer based on T5‑base with a newly initialized head that predicts the mean of the noise to be added in embedding space.
What is the total score achieved by PENTESTGPT in the picoMini CTF competition?
PENTESTGPT accumulated 1400 points out of a possible 4200.
What average ROC‑AUC did BAIT achieve across all evaluated LLMs?
BAIT achieved an average ROC‑AUC of 0.98 across the evaluated models, significantly higher than the baselines’ average of 0.61.
How does I‑FSJ handle perturbation-based defenses like Retokenization?
Retokenization with 20 % dropout reduces ASR to about 48–56 % rule‑based, but I‑FSJ still achieves around 90 % ASR when using eight shots and demo‑level random search.
How many epochs does the MLP classifier train for during A1 and A2 attacks?
The classifier is trained for six epochs in both A1 and A2 scenarios.
What is the primary objective of Token Highlighter?
Token Highlighter aims to detect and mitigate jailbreak threats in user queries by identifying critical tokens that influence affirmative responses and reducing their impact through Soft Removal.
Which models were evaluated with bijection learning in the main experiments?
The evaluation included Claude 3 Haiku, Claude 3 Opus, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, GPT‑4o‑mini, and GPT‑4o.
Which type of attack was identified as capable of answering all target queries at half the detail of an undefended model in the last-generation system?
Four jailbreaks were capable of answering all target queries at half the detail, with an average discovery time of 27 hours.
What overarching recommendation does the paper give for improving quantitative AI red teaming practices?

Researchers should clearly define and systematize what is being measured, ensure conceptual coherence by comparing compatible estimands, validate measurement instruments against true success criteria, and report judge performance disaggregated across systems and methods to mitigate bias.
In MuJoCo Hopper experiments, what success rate did the best gradient‑based attack achieve with a 10% budget?
Approximately 90% success rate was achieved when flipping 10% of labels in Hopper.
What is the purpose of the “NotEnoughInformationError” in CaMeL’s design?

The Q-LLM raises this error when it lacks sufficient data to fulfill a parsing request. CaMeL then stops execution and asks the P-LLM to generate new code that provides the missing context, preventing the Q-LLM from revealing what information is missing.
How many layers exhibit a peak in effective rank during safety fine‑tuning according to the study?
The effective rank peaks around layer 20 for both SSFT and DPO, indicating increased diversity of directions at that depth.
How many initialization starts are used when aligning ADC’s cost with GCG’s for a fair comparison?
Sixteen independent starts are run in parallel, matching GCG’s computational budget and achieving 93% of its cost.
Which defense variant provides the strongest protection against I‑FSJ on Llama‑2‑Chat?
The SmoothLLM swap variant with 20 % perturbation reduces ASR to about 56 % rule‑based and 86 % LLM‑based, but I‑FSJ still achieves over 85 % ASR with eight shots.
What is the compute overhead reduction factor achieved by the two-stage cascade compared to the single exchange classifier?
The two-stage cascade reduces computational cost by a factor of 5.4 relative to the single exchange classifier.
What is the maximum number of iterations (T) used in Algorithm 1 for LLMO?
The algorithm specifies a maximum iteration count T, but the exact numeric value is not provided; it is determined by the experiment’s fitness evaluation limit.
What is the overall F1‑score achieved by OCR‑APT on the DARPA TC3 CADETS host?
OCR‑APT achieves an F1‑score of 1.00, indicating perfect precision and recall for that host under the evaluation protocol.
What tool does the agent use to check completion of each sub‑task during attack construction?
The agent employs standard tool‑use APIs that provide file read/write, code execution, and state verification functions for each step.
How does the paper define Attack Success Rate (ASR)?
ASR is the percentage of malicious prompts that elicit misaligned outputs, measured by a classifier trained on the HH‑RLHF dataset to detect unsafe responses.
How does the model’s ASR change when safety‑critical ranks are removed while keeping utility ranks untouched in Llama2‑13B‑chat?
Removing up to 120 orthogonally projected safety ranks increases ASR close to 1, with average accuracy remaining above 0.5.
How does R 2‑Guard perform against the GCG‑U1 universal jailbreak compared to other models?
R 2‑Guard maintains a 100% unsafety detection rate (UDR) for GCG‑U1, outperforming all baselines which drop below 80%.
What is the average attack success rate (ASR) achieved by JudgeDeceiver on Mistral-7B with MT-Bench?
The average ASR is 90.8% across the evaluated question‑response pairs.
In which metric does PLeak achieve a score of 1.00 against the GPT‑J model on the Financial dataset?
The Exact Match (EM) accuracy reaches 1.00 when attacking GPT‑J with PLeak on the Financial dataset.
What is the average accuracy achieved by the largest GPT‑3 model (175 billion parameters) on this benchmark?
The X‑Large GPT‑3 model attains an overall accuracy of approximately 43.9 %.
Explain how target tokens \(V_t\) are selected during poison prompt generation.
Top‑k candidates from the language model head’s softmax over the [MASK] position are retrieved, excluding any token already present in the clean label set.
What is the measured toxicity rate for RealToxicPrompts when ChatGPT operates under default settings?
The toxicity rate was 0.005, indicating that only 0.5% of outputs were classified as toxic in the default setting.
How many distinct safety‑related prompts were used to test input manipulation before and after prompt injection?
100 unsafe prompts were tested; only 2 received direct responses initially, but 95 were answered after prompt injection.
How does ADC transform a dense vector into an S‑sparse vector during optimization?
ADC applies Algorithm 1: it zeroes all but the top‑S elements of each vector, adds a small epsilon for numerical stability, then normalizes to sum to one.
What is the role of the Langevin dynamics step size η in COLD-Attack?
The step size η controls how far logits move during each iteration; a larger η can accelerate convergence but may overshoot, while a smaller η ensures finer updates.
What key question is highlighted in the pre‑activity phase regarding the artifact under evaluation?
A key question asks which version of the model (including fine‑tuning details) is to be evaluated and what safety guardrails are already in place for that artifact.
What is the maximum batch size used in the experiments?
A maximum running batch size of 16 was set during evaluation.
How does the paper describe the relationship between red‑teaming and other evaluation methods like audits or impact assessments?

The paper positions red‑teaming as one of several complementary evaluation paradigms, noting that it cannot alone guarantee safety and should be combined with audits, impact assessments, and other techniques.
What is the computational complexity of CODEBREAKER compared to LM Memorization and CodexLeaks?
While both baseline methods run in O(n) time for n responses, CODEBREAKER’s dynamic mutation adds a factor M (iterations), yielding O(M · n) which remains practically linear as M is small.
How many datasets were used to evaluate TAP, and what are they called?
Two datasets were used: the AdvBench Subset with 50 harmful prompts across 32 categories, and a new dataset of 123 harmful requests generated by WizardVicuna30B-Uncensored.
What dataset is used to generate harmful prompts for evaluating jailbreak attacks?
The AdvBench Harmful Behaviors dataset is used to provide harmful requests and target harmful strings for jailbreak evaluation.
How does the Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) loss differ from standard SFT in SEAS training?

DPO simultaneously increases the probability of chosen outputs while decreasing that of rejected ones, leading to faster improvement after one epoch compared to SFT, which only reinforces correct answers and may stagnate.
What is the purpose of the system prompt filter component in the GUARDIAN framework?
It screens incoming prompts for malicious content before they reach the LLM, helping neutralize prompt injection attacks.
What is the overall conclusion about Gradient Cuff’s effectiveness and utility trade‑off?

Gradient Cuff significantly improves jailbreak detection across diverse attacks while keeping false positives low; its utility impact is modest compared to other defenses, making it a practical choice for protecting aligned LLMs.
Which general LLMs were included in the evaluation and what is their highest reported ASR for the Level I attack?
GPT‑3.5, GPT‑4, and GPT‑4o were evaluated; GPT‑3.5 shows a 68.3% ASR while GPT‑4 and GPT‑4o have lower rates of 23.8% and 36.5% respectively.
What is the role of the “Sure, I'd like to help you with this” sentence in Token Highlighter?
This sentence serves as a fixed affirmation target; its loss guides the calculation of token influence and helps locate tokens that push the model toward affirmative responses.
What dataset is used for training and evaluating the adversarial perturbations?
The Microsoft COCO 2014 dataset is used, with splits of 113,287 training images, 5,000 validation images, and 5,000 test images.
What is the step size applied to perturb the image during optimization?
The image perturbation step size is set to 1/255.
Which three vulnerabilities does BADROBOT exploit in embodied LLM systems?

BADROBOT exploits (i) manipulation of the LLM within robotic systems, (ii) misalignment between linguistic outputs and physical actions, and (iii) unintentional hazardous behaviors caused by flaws in world knowledge.
Which baseline methods were compared against JailFuzzer, and what was a key difference in their approach?

SneakyPrompt, Ring‑A‑Bell, and DACA were compared; unlike these token or prompt‑level methods that often produce unnatural prompts, JailFuzzer uses fuzzing with LLM agents to generate natural language jailbreaks.
What is the impact of older library versions (e.g., TensorFlow 1) on attack feasibility?
Defenses implemented with outdated libraries pose significant challenges; no model successfully attacked any defense using TensorFlow 1 due to missing functions and incompatibilities.
What is the computational cost of the Conceptual Deception attack in terms of API queries?
B cd requires one API query per malicious command for semantic re‑phrasing, yielding a time complexity of O(1) per query and negligible additional overhead.
What perturbation instruction is used to add extraneous characters at the end of a sentence?
Instruction C3 instructs the LLM to “Add at most two extraneous characters to the end of the sentence.”
How many high-risk vulnerabilities were discovered during the 1,736 hours of red‑teaming for the production‑grade system?
Only one high‑risk vulnerability was found, corresponding to a discovery rate of 0.005 per thousand queries.
How does JailFuzzer’s query efficiency compare to SneakyPrompt when attacking text‑match filters?
JailFuzzer uses roughly the same number of queries as SneakyPrompt for text‑match filters, but achieves higher overall success rates.
How many tokens were used as a maximum length for filtering sequences before embedding?
Sequences longer than 32 tokens were discarded to avoid issues with embedding large texts.
By what percentage did the integrated similarity approach generate more adversarial samples compared to cosine similarity?
The integrated similarity approach generated 30.4% more adversarial samples than the cosine similarity approach.
Why does LLMO avoid transfer functions used by other metaheuristics?

Transfer functions can map distinct solutions in the original domain to identical ones in the transformed space, reducing offspring diversity and search efficiency; LLMO directly encodes solutions, eliminating this issue.
Which defense mechanism was shown to significantly reduce GCG’s effectiveness but not AutoDAN’s?
A perplexity‑based defense that rejects prompts exceeding a threshold; GCG’s gibberish prompts trigger it, while AutoDAN’s semantically meaningful prompts bypass it.
How does the algorithm determine which token position to perturb first?
It samples candidate tokens, substitutes each at every mask position, fine‑tunes on the resulting set, and selects the position whose average loss is minimal across candidates.
How does I‑FSJ handle the presence of a system prompt in Llama‑2‑Chat?
I‑FSJ injects the [/INST] token that aligns with the system prompt’s formatting, ensuring the model interprets the injected demonstration as part of the conversation context.
In the context of code generation, what specific vulnerability is introduced by entropy reduction bias?
The attack forces the model to select a random seed from a limited set (e.g., 1000 values), drastically reducing password entropy and making generated passwords predictable.
How does the paper justify using a finite‑sample approximation for the attack objective?
A theoretical analysis shows that with polynomial sample complexity, solving the finite‑sample proxy yields solutions within ε of the true bi‑level optimization with high probability.
Which category of harmful content did Amazon Q detect early and terminate code completions for during evaluation?
Amazon Q frequently terminated completions early for the pornography category, indicating stronger proactive detection for that type of content.
How many prompt templates were evaluated in the study?
Five different prompt templates were tested on SST‑2 to assess their impact on attack performance.
What assumption links the concept to the support of the ground‑truth distribution?

Assumption 2.1 states that for any plausible prompts sharing the same concept \(c\), the support of the ground‑truth distribution is identical: \(\text{supp}(p_{\text{world}}(q,c))=\text{supp}(p_{\text{world}}(q^*,c))\).
What prompting strategy does OCR‑APT employ to reduce hallucinations during report generation?
OCR‑APT uses a multi‑stage Chain‑of‑Thought (CoT) prompting pipeline that separates IOC extraction, stage mapping, and context summarization into distinct validated subtasks.
Which models were evaluated using logprob access in the study?

Models with full logprob access include Vicuna‑13B, Mistral‑7B, Phi‑3‑Mini, Nemotron‑4‑340B, Llama‑2‑Chat (all sizes), Llama‑3‑Instruct‑8B, Gemma‑7B, GPT‑3.5 Turbo, GPT‑4o, and the adversarially trained R2D2.
What is the average harmfulness score (HS) achieved by the evolved persona prompts alone on GPT‑4o-mini?
The HS averages around 1.85, indicating low perceived harm when used in isolation.
What happens to LLM rater accuracy when the comment length exceeds 100 characters?
Accuracy declines for longer comments because the model may miss short violative passages within a larger benign context; segmentation could mitigate this but adds cost.
What role does σ‑similarity play in Theorem 2’s bound?
σ‑similarity bounds the variance of log likelihood ratios between components; it contributes a √|s₀| term scaled by σ in the misalignment prompt length.
Which decoding strategies are evaluated for their impact on PLeak’s success?
Beam‑search, sampling, and beam‑sample decoding strategies are examined for how they affect attack metrics.
Which safety filter type shows the lowest re‑use bypass rate in the experiments?
Image‑based safety filters, because random seeds cause different generated images for the same prompt.
Which defense mechanism is more effective against JailPO’s TemplatePrompt attacks?
The LLM-Guard defense shows a higher DPR for TemplatePrompt compared to Perplexity, indicating it better detects complex scenario prompts.
Which platform contributed the largest share of jailbreak prompts after September 2023?
Prompt‑aggregation websites, especially FlowGPT, supplied more than seventy‑five percent of jailbreak prompts in the months following September 2023.
Which two datasets are used for training and evaluating the red‑teaming methods?
The Red Teaming dataset, containing dialogues between a human red‑teamer and an AI assistant, and the alpaca‑gpt4 dataset, which consists of instruction‑following tasks generated by GPT‑4.
What recommendation does the paper give for improving transparency in red‑teaming reports?
The paper recommends that reports include resource consumption, success assessment against predefined goals, mitigation steps taken, and any subsequent evaluation of the artifact.
What is the main goal of the CODEBREAKER framework?

The main goal of CODEBREAKER is to automatically extract personal information (PI) from code language models by maximizing PI concentration and interconnection within a limited number of model responses.
Which metric indicates a model’s helpfulness after applying countermeasures?
The MT‑Bench score measures multi‑turn conversation quality and instruction following ability, reflecting helpfulness.
Which LLM had a 50% success rate for prompt leaking attacks?
Gemini achieved a 50% partial response rate and a 50% safety trigger rate in leaking tests.
How does Conceptual Deception (B cd) manipulate an embodied LLM’s behavior?
B cd uses semantic re‑phrasing to transform overtly malicious commands into seemingly benign ones that still trigger harmful actions.
Which benchmark shows that SEAS improves safety without sacrificing general capability?
The MT‑Bench and Arena Hard benchmarks demonstrate stable or improved scores for SEAS Target models across iterations, indicating preserved general performance.
What datasets were used for evaluating jailbreak attacks in the study?
The experiments employed two widely recognized datasets: AdvBench, containing 520 harmful or toxic examples, and MaliciousInstruct, consisting of 100 malicious instructions across ten categories.
How does CaMeL extract control flow from a natural language prompt?

The P-LLM writes a short Python program that outlines the sequence of actions required to fulfill the user’s request. This code is then interpreted by CaMeL’s custom interpreter to track dependencies and enforce policies.
How does SysVec handle adjustments to a system prompt that only change slightly?
It can initialize a new SysVec from the previous one and fine‑tune on the modified prompt, reducing training time by about 46% compared to random initialization.
Which parameter controls how many tokens an attacker can request per response in the experiments?
The maximum output token length is set to 128 for each request during evaluation.
Why does providing the paper describing a defense sometimes reduce an LLM’s success rate?
Including the paper can distract the model or cause it to rely on irrelevant textual details, leading to earlier termination of useful attack steps.
What role does the dummy request play during token extraction?
Dummy requests are sent before and after candidate tokens to fill the queue, ensuring that only cache hits alter the return order, providing a side‑channel signal.
In what way does JAM differ from earlier jailbreak methods like GCG or PAIR?

Unlike prior methods that focus solely on input manipulation, JAM explicitly targets output‑level moderation by inserting cipher characters, enabling it to bypass both input and output guardrails simultaneously.
What decoding strategy was used to confirm that the trend of increasing unsafe rates with decreasing language resources holds under nucleus sampling?
Nucleus sampling with a top‑p value of 0.8 was applied, and results showed an increased unsafe rate (1.25% higher than temperature = 0) while maintaining the same ranking order.
What temperature was used for sampling in the PPO policy during instruction‑following experiments?
A temperature of 0.7 was used for all methods in those experiments.
What proportion of the training data was used for pre‑training each HDC model?
Seventy percent of the training data in each dataset was used for pre‑training, leaving thirty percent as validation data for adversarial generation.
Which activation functions are used to create the skewed model in Stage 1 of SKEWACT?
The skewed model is created by replacing each original activation function with either ReLU or LeakyReLU, depending on a hyper‑parameter choice.
Which two target models were evaluated in Experiment I?
The experiments targeted gpt‑3.5‑turbo‑0125 and Llama‑2‑7B‑Chat, both of which had no successful jailbreaks from the seed templates.
What is the role of the Debias LoRA Block in LLMGuardrail?
The Debias LoRA Block replaces original intermediate states with debiased ones using low‑rank adaptation matrices, reducing bias while keeping semantic information intact during inference.
What is the average FID score achieved by SneakyPrompt‑RL when bypassing Stable Diffusion’s original safety filter in one‑time attacks?
Approximately 132.01, indicating good semantic similarity to target images.
In the pre‑filtering mode, what recall threshold yields the highest removal of non‑violative content for election misinformation?
A 95.1% recall threshold allows the LLM to filter out 99.3% of non‑violative comments while still passing almost all violations to human raters.
How does the style subspace diversity reward differ from overall diversity measurement?
It projects attack embeddings onto a space orthogonal to goal embeddings, focusing similarity on stylistic features rather than content.
What is the “ε‑bounded adversary” introduced in Section 4?

An ε‑bounded adversary can perturb the query part of a prompt so that the resulting output distribution moves at most ε (in a chosen distance metric such as ℓ₁, TV, or JSD) toward the harmful zone on the probability simplex.
What happens to harmlessness on clean prompts when the model is poisoned and fine‑tuned with PPO?
The average reward for clean prompts remains largely unchanged, preserving safe behavior in non‑triggered contexts.
What threshold does the second-stage external classifier use to achieve a 0.1% refusal rate on WildChat when run independently?
The second-stage classifier is calibrated so that its independent operation yields a 0.1% refusal rate on WildChat.
Which real‑world agent was successfully attacked using UDora’s transferability approach?
Perplexity AI’s Web Search Agent Sonar, where adversarial strings from AgentHarm were reused to induce malicious searches.
What is the main benefit of integrating multiple safety evaluators into a single toolkit like JailbreakEval?
It provides flexibility, standardization, and the ability to compare different evaluation methods within a unified framework.
What is the computational cost per token for a full-layer linear probe on Gemma 3‑27B compared to a single-token forward pass through the same model?
The probe requires about 377k FLOPs per token, whereas a single-token forward pass costs roughly 8 billion FLOPs, making the probe several orders of magnitude cheaper.
What LoRA hyperparameters are used during SecAlign training?
The LoRA rank r is set to 64, lora_alpha to 8, lora_dropout to 0.1, and target modules include “q_proj” and “v_proj”.
Which LLMs were used as targets for evaluating jailbreak prompts?
GPT‑3.5, GPT‑4, and PaLM‑2 were tested via their official APIs.
What is the Attack Success Rate (ASR) achieved by the baseline Llama model on the StrongReject-small dataset?
The baseline ASR for Llama on StrongReject-small is 2%.
What is the primary detector used to judge jailbreak success?
ClassJudge, a RoBERTa‑based binary classifier, evaluates whether a response correctly answers the prompt.
What is the computational cost implication of using Debias LoRA compared to standard LoRA?
Debias LoRA replaces rather than adds residuals, resulting in minimal additional parameters (rank‑m matrices) and negligible inference overhead while providing debiased representations.
What is the role of the “Jailbreak Checker” component in the system framework?

The Jailbreak Checker uses the set of checker prompts, the victim LLM’s response, and the user question to judge whether the prompt has jailbreak intent by comparing likelihoods from normal and jailbreak prompt sets.
What is the impact of retention rate on reconstruction attack performance according to Table 6?
Higher retention rates lead to better extraction accuracy; as the proportion of preserved content decreases from 75% to 25%, BLEU and embedding scores drop across all methods.
What three attack baselines were compared against ProAttacker?
PWWS, TextFooler, and CLARE were used as baseline black‑box adversarial attacks for comparison.
How does a String Matching evaluator determine if an answer is safe?
It checks for the absence of predefined refusal phrases such as “I am sorry” or “I cannot” in the model’s response; if none are found, the answer is considered unsafe.
Which training technique ensures that content is considered harmful only when the probe consistently predicts harm across consecutive tokens?
Sliding Window Mean (SWiM) logit smoothing averages logits over a window of tokens, ensuring consistent harmful predictions before flagging an exchange.
How does CaMeL’s approach to capability tagging compare to traditional software security systems?
It mirrors concepts like libcap, Capsicum, and CHERI by attaching fine‑grained tags (capabilities) to data values, allowing precise control over who can read or transform each piece of information.
Which advanced LLMs were tested beyond the baseline models, and what was a key finding?
Llama3-70B, GPT‑4, and Command-R were tested; QEPrompt achieved higher ASR on Llama3-70B, indicating scalability to larger models.
Which LLM had a 100% pass rate for prompt leaking attacks according to Table VI?
ChatSonic achieved a 100% full response rate in the prompt leaking experiments.
How does RL+TDiv differ from RL+Curiosity in terms of what it maximizes?
RL+TDiv maximizes diversity of the target model’s response embeddings, while RL+Curiosity maximizes novelty of the prompts themselves.
What is the purpose of the attack objective (AO) in PromptAttack’s prompt?
AO instructs the LLM to generate a new sentence that keeps semantic meaning but causes misclassification by the victim model.
Which baseline shows the lowest cosine similarity when ε=0.5 for Vicuna‑7b?
The few‑shot baseline has a cosine similarity of 0.40, indicating larger deviations from the reference prompt compared to DART (0.74).
Which trigger type achieved the highest average attack success rate on LLaVA?
The combined OCR text and visual trigger achieved the highest average ASR of 0.870 for LLaVA.
How many tokens are typically replaced in a single adversarial prompt generated by SneakyPrompt‑RL?
Usually one sensitive token is replaced with three non‑sensitive tokens (m = 3).
In what way does R 2‑Guard’s PC inference achieve a precision‑efficiency balance?
By optimizing the graph structure—clustering variables and layering inference—to maintain high moderation accuracy while reducing computation time.
What precision and recall values did HATEGUARD attain on the “Mask” category during Q2 (April‑June)?
Precision was 0.99 and recall was 0.96 for Mask‑related hate in that quarter.
Which two attack scenarios are defined for MODX?
Scenario #1 focuses on converting benign subjects into NSFW genres (NSFW genre transfer), and Scenario #2 involves generating NSFW images with inherently sensitive subjects (NSFW content generation).
How does AutoDAN handle mutation during genetic evolution?
With a 1% probability, it applies LLM‑based diversification to replace a prompt, preserving global meaning while adding diversity.
Describe the incremental search technique used by PLeak.
Incremental search optimizes an AQ in stages, first targeting short prefixes of shadow system prompts and gradually increasing the length, thereby reducing local optima and improving convergence.
In the multi‑step RL setting, what additional reward component is introduced?
A diversity reward (R_Div) that penalizes similarity to past attacks in the same trajectory, promoting stylistic variety.
Which two attack goals are formally defined for SFT data extraction in the study?
The two goals are reconstruction attacks, which aim to recover the exact original SFT data, and retraining attacks, which use extracted data to fine‑tune a new model with comparable performance.
What is the overall refusal rate on production traffic reported for the last-generation system?
The last-generation system had a refusal rate of 0.073% on production traffic.
How does the study define a "full response" from an LLM to a jailbreak prompt?
A full response occurs when the LLM answers the malicious prompt and produces output exactly as intended by the attacker.
In what way does the Self‑Reminder system prompt differ from the default Llama‑2 system prompt?
Self‑Reminder adds an explicit instruction for responsible behavior, reinforcing safety guidelines beyond the standard Llama‑2 prompt.
How many candidate tokens are considered during each optimization round before filtering in SKEWACT?
The method considers a top‑64 set of token candidates per position before applying robust filtering.
In the SEP benchmark, how does SecAlign’s utility compare to the undefended model?
SecAlign retains about 46.6% of the utility relative to the undefended model, a substantial improvement over StruQ’s 18.9%.
How does the paper suggest handling differential judge error across target systems?
By measuring and reporting judge performance disaggregated by target system and jailbreak method, and applying statistical corrections if significant differential errors are detected.
What is the default temperature setting used for the target LM during BEAST attacks?
The temperature is set to 1.0 for all BEAST experiments.
How does the use of machine translation affect the unsafe rate compared to human translation in the unintentional scenario?
Machine translation yields a slightly higher average unsafe rate (11.15%) than human translation (10.19%), indicating that native speakers are not strictly necessary for effective jailbreaks.
How many stages of an APT kill‑chain does the LLM investigator aim to map in its reports?

The investigator maps all standard MITRE ATT&CK stages: Initial Compromise, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, Command & Control, Internal Reconnaissance, Lateral Movement, Data Exfiltration, and Covering Tracks.
What is the reported accuracy of a clean 7B reward model on the harmless test set before poisoning?
Approximately 73% accuracy on safe completions receiving higher rewards than unsafe ones.
Describe the bi‑level optimization framework used by POISONPROMPT.
The lower level optimizes prompt tuning on clean data to maintain task performance, while the upper level trains a backdoor trigger that causes the model to output target tokens when triggered.
What is one limitation of the study related to data collection for RLHF?
The study uses pre‑collected Anthropic data rather than real‑time human annotations, limiting exploration of adversarial prompt selection during training.
Which three open‑source LLMs are tested in the experiments?
Llama2-7B, Mistral-7B, and Vicuna-7B are the three open‑source models evaluated.
How does dynamic few‑shot example selection affect performance compared to hand‑picked examples?
Dynamic selection generally improves accuracy for most policies, especially violent extremism and harassment with text‑unicorn, but can degrade performance if labels are noisy.
What does the projection value distribution reveal after non‑dominant suppression in early layers?
Post‑suppression projections are distinctly separated from pre‑suppression values, indicating a clear shift in activation space caused by removing indirect safety directions.
What metric is used to evaluate harmfulness in the LLM‑based ASR calculation?
The evaluation uses the Llama Guard classifier, which labels responses as safe or unsafe based on predefined content categories such as violence, hate, sexual content, self‑harm, and misinformation.
What is the refusal rate reported for the last-generation system on production traffic?
The last-generation system had a refusal rate of 0.073% on production traffic.
What advantage does the MixAsking pattern have over QEPrompt alone?
MixAsking balances high‑risk output generation with defense evasion, achieving higher Question Success Rate (QSR) while keeping query count low.
According to the evaluation, how does GPT‑4‑VISION perform on detecting invalid images compared to LLAVA‑13B?
GPT‑4‑VISION correctly flagged all invalid images without needing post‑processing, while LLAVA‑13B required additional formatting steps but also detected all invalid inputs.
What is the key observation about sequence embeddings that enables the first attack?

Intermediate sequence embeddings at a given layer cluster primarily by their ending token, with minimal influence from preceding tokens, allowing a classifier to predict the last token of each embedding.
Which baseline attack uses a genetic algorithm to optimize jailbreak prompt generation?
AutoDAN employs a genetic algorithm to generate and refine jailbreak prompts.
What metric is used to report agreement between an automated evaluator and human judgments in the meta‑evaluation?
Cohen’s Kappa coefficient is reported as the primary agreement metric.
What is the significance of the ratio β′/β in the theoretical bounds?

The ratio β′/β (≈ 2–3) determines how much larger the upper KL bound can be compared to the lower bound, influencing the length of misaligning prompts needed when components are not perfectly distinguishable.
Which adaptive attack method achieves 100 % success on GPT‑4o?
A custom prompt combined with random search and self‑transfer yields a 100 % success rate on GPT‑4o.
What is the purpose of the “β × x′i” operation in Soft Removal?
Multiplying each selected token’s embedding by β shrinks its influence proportionally, reducing its contribution to the model’s output while keeping the token present.
According to the experiments, how does RLHF affect distinguishability compared to a pretrained model?
RLHF increases the distinguishability β (e.g., β ≈ 20 for LLaMA 2 13B chat) relative to pretrained models where β is about five times smaller (~4).
Which models were used as base models for the GPT‑Recheck metric?
GPT‑4‑0613 was used both for rechecking responses and for LLM‑based diversification during AutoDAN’s execution.
Which evaluation metric indicates how many queries were needed to compromise all questions?
The Attack Success Rate (ASR) reflects the proportion of total queries that successfully jailbreak each question.
Which embedding model is chosen for indexing log documents in the vector store?
The “textembedding‑3‑large” model from OpenAI is selected due to its superior performance on textual similarity tasks.
Which models were adversarially trained against the GCG attack and still vulnerable to the new attacks?
R2D2, a model adversarially trained against GCG, remains fully vulnerable, achieving 100 % success with the new adaptive methods.
What metric is used to assess whether a model’s response fulfills a malicious instruction?
The ASR‑G metric, which employs GPT‑4 to judge compliance with the harmful instruction, is used.
What metric is used to evaluate the naturalness of generated jailbreak prompts, and how did JailFuzzer score on it?
Perplexity (PPL) measured by GPT‑2 was used; JailFuzzer achieved low PPL scores (e.g., 30.81 for SD1.4), indicating highly natural prompts compared to SneakyPrompt’s high PPL.
Which LLM achieved a 75% attack success rate on the CTF‑like subset?
Claude 3.5 Sonnet reached a 75% success rate, successfully attacking 18 out of 24 defenses in that subset.
What is the average number of queries required to jailbreak gpt‑3.5‑turbo‑0125 using LLM-FUZZER?
On average, about 225 queries were needed per unethical question to find a successful jailbreak prompt for gpt‑3.5‑turbo‑0125.
How does BEAST handle the system prompt (x(s₂)) in its attack pipeline?
After generating the adversarial suffix, BEAST concatenates it with the original system prompts and user prompt before feeding the full sequence into the LM for evaluation against the objective.
Which non‑dominant direction was found to regulate PAIR jailbreaks specifically?
The sixth component of layer 14 (L14‑C6) activates on patterns like “Sure, I’m happy to help” combined with “Imagine”, and its removal reduces refusal for PAIR prompts.
What is the purpose of the “Prompt Dilution” technique in T2I jailbreak attacks?
Prompt Dilution adds unrelated text to a prompt to degrade filter performance, making it harder for safety mechanisms to detect malicious content.
Which four categories of COVID-19–related online hate were specifically analyzed?
Anti‑Asian, Ageism, Mask, and Vaccine.
What is the role of beam search in the third phase of A3?
Beam search fills in missing tokens by generating candidate sequences constrained to top‑5 nearest‑neighbor candidates, guided by a shadow LLM’s probability outputs.
How does the paper handle attacks that exceed the maximum token length during training?
They receive a penalty from the length reward, which reduces their overall reward and discourages overly long outputs.
What is the ASR achieved by I‑FSJ on Llama‑2‑7B‑Chat without any defense?
I‑FSJ achieves over 95 % rule‑based and 96 % LLM‑based ASRs on Llama‑2‑7B‑Chat when using four shots with special tokens injected and demo‑level random search.
What is the primary benefit of using pre‑training with human feedback (PHF) as a defense?
PHF reduces unwanted content in outputs and increases user satisfaction compared to standard fine‑tuning alone.
What are the two large language models examined in this study?
GPT-J, a 6‑billion parameter transformer with 28 layers, and Mamba‑2, a state‑space model with 64 layers.
In the Safety Misalignment attack, what happens when the LLM generates a safe language response but an unsafe action plan?
The system outputs a refusal in natural language while still encoding the malicious command in the structured function list, leading to execution of harmful actions.
What is the batch size used during supervised fine‑tuning of the attack models?
A batch size of four tokens is employed for supervised fine‑tuning.
In the conversation scenario, what is the key insight of Theorem 3?

Theorem 3 indicates that during a multi‑turn dialogue, an adversarial user must insert enough misaligning text in each turn; otherwise, the LLM’s responses can counteract misalignment, requiring total prompt length to exceed a bound involving β′, σ, and the number of turns.
What is the calibration gap observed between GPT‑3’s confidence and its actual accuracy on the benchmark?
GPT‑3’s confidence can be off by up to 24 % from its true accuracy, indicating significant miscalibration.
What role does the “user‑to‑assistant” residual‑stream vector play in the toy model explaining power laws?

The vector represents aggregated information from each demonstration; its mean and covariance determine how the probability of attack success scales with the number of shots, leading to a −1 exponent.
What hyperparameter controls the discount factor in multi‑step RL training?
The discount γ is set to 0, meaning only immediate rewards influence each step.
How many epochs were used to fine‑tune ChatGPT with SELF‑DEFENCE training data?
Three epochs were used for fine‑tuning ChatGPT with the SELF‑DEFENCE dataset.
How does Prompt-Crossing compute the probability that a response comes from normal versus jailbreak prompts?

It calculates cross‑entropy loss for each checker prompt combined with the user question and response, then averages likelihoods over all normal or jailbreak checker prompts to estimate the relative probability.
What are the three explicit side‑channel attack examples discussed for CaMeL?

1) Indirect inference via tool calls that depend on a private variable. 2) Exception‑based leakage of one bit by triggering errors conditioned on private data. 3) Timing attacks that remove annotations from private variables during execution.
Which benchmark suite caused the highest policy denial rate in CaMeL’s evaluation?
The Slack suite had the highest denial rate because it contains many untrusted web‑page sources and lacks clear user identity information, leading to frequent security policy blocks.
What is generation‑aware alignment and how does it differ from fixed‑decoding alignment?

Generation‑aware alignment collects training examples generated under multiple decoding strategies to expose the model to diverse outputs before fine‑tuning, whereas fixed‑decoding alignment uses only a single decoding setting during training.
What is the definition of the refusal loss function ϕθ(x) for a query x?

The refusal loss ϕθ(x) is defined as the probability that the model will produce a non‑refusal response to x, computed as the expectation over the indicator JB(y) that checks whether the generated reply y triggers a refusal.
Which two large language models were evaluated for defense effectiveness?
The experiments used the open‑source Llama‑2‑7B‑Chat model and the closed‑source GPT‑3.5‑Turbo model.
Which defense uses swap perturbations with a 10% perturbation percentage?
SmoothLLM employs swap perturbations with q = 10%.
What is the maximum number of queries per loop (Θ) used in the default configuration, and how does it affect performance?
Θ = (4, 10, 10, …) is used; this setting yields a 100% bypass rate while keeping average queries low at about 7.04.
What is the reported failure mode for the "Prompt Too Long" category in YouChat and Copilot?
The "Prompt Too Long" issue is most significant in YouChat and Copilot, especially in English and Arabic prompts.
What temperature and top‑p settings were used during the inference of target LLMs in experiments?
All target LLMs were run with a temperature of 0 and top‑p of 1 (i.e., deterministic generation).
How does ADC perform on Vicuna-v1.5-7B in the AdvBench harmful strings benchmark compared to GCG?
ADC achieves an EM of 99.8% versus GCG’s 99.0%, showing superior precision in output generation.
What metric is reported to evaluate attack effectiveness across all datasets?
Attack Success Rate (ASR), defined as the proportion of cases where the agent performs the targeted malicious action.
What is the trade‑off observed when increasing the proportion of unsafe training data in SELF‑DEFENCE from 0 % to 100 %?

Increasing unsafe training data improves safety (unsafe rates drop from 82.33% to 97.67% in unintentional scenarios) but reduces usefulness, with accuracy on XNLI and X‑CSQA decreasing from 40% to 23.33%.
What is the Attack Success Rate (ASR) definition used in jailbreak evaluation?

The ASR measures the proportion of successful jailbreak attempts among all prohibited queries, calculated as the number of attempts where the evaluator returns 1 divided by the total number of attempts.
What training time advantage does DART have over the auto‑regressive model for Vicuna‑7b?
DART requires approximately 3 hours and 0 minutes, whereas the auto‑regressive baseline needs about 4 hours and 52 minutes.
In what scenario does BAIT terminate the generation of a candidate sequence early based on self‑entropy?

If the self‑entropy at a step exceeds the upper threshold \( \phi_2 \), indicating uniform probability distribution, BAIT stops exploring that sequence as it is unlikely to contain the backdoor target.
What is the size of the new dataset collected for studying new waves of online hate?
The dataset contains 31,549 tweets related to three recent new waves of online hate.
What role do special control tokens play in the chat template structure?
Tokens like BOT (<|im start|>), EOT (<|im end|>), and role identifiers (user, assistant) delimit turns and roles, guiding the model’s generation flow.
What is the purpose of red teaming as described in the paper?
Red teaming systematically attacks LLMs to identify harmful outputs and strengthen defenses.
How does the second adversary (A2) handle mismatched auxiliary data distributions?

A2 synthesizes synthetic embeddings for unseen tokens by generating random token sequences that end with target tokens, then augments the training set with these synthetic pairs to improve generalization.
What is the main difference between prompt‑level and token‑level jailbreak attacks described in the paper?
Prompt‑level attacks rely on social engineering and semantic manipulation of entire prompts, while token‑level attacks involve inserting or adjusting specific tokens to bypass safety filters.
How many preference pairs are constructed for the Template Enhanced Model (TEM)?
TEM’s preference dataset contains 2,580 pairwise preferences derived from 10 responses per template and question set.
Which SVD component explains the largest proportion of variance in the LAT model’s activation differences?
The first SVD component accounts for more than 54% of the total variance in LAT.
Which task category has the highest number of tasks in the benchmark?
The STEM category contains the largest share of tasks among the four broad disciplines.
How many distinct personas were tested to generate toxic language in personalized dialogue?
Two personas—Joffrey Baratheon and Eric Cartman—were used to demonstrate toxic outputs through personalization.
Which baseline moderation tools were evaluated against new waves of online hate?
Clarifai Text Moderation, Perspective API, Azure Text Moderation, and IBM Toxic Comment Classifier.
Which prompt injection scenario led ChatGPT to produce instructions for committing murder?
By asking the model to "write a poem about the way to commit a murder," ChatGPT generated a poem containing instructions for murder, bypassing its safety response.
What human agreement percentage did the classifier achieve when compared to annotators on alignment judgments?
The classifier achieved 92% agreement with human judgments, indicating high reliability in detecting misaligned outputs.
What adversarial attack methods were considered in the experiments?
The study used fast gradient sign method (FGSM), projected gradient descent (PGD), adaptive PGD (APGD), and square attack.
How many iterations (L) does BEAST run for jailbreak attacks, and why is this number chosen?
BEAST runs 40 iterations (L = 40) for jailbreaks to balance between achieving high ASR and maintaining a short runtime.
How does the paper’s defense method modify token logits to mitigate DDE attacks?

The defense increases the logit of the highest‑probability token so that its probability matches a randomly chosen target between τ and 1, while leaving other logits unchanged, thereby obscuring low‑confidence tokens.
Which dataset contains 939 prompts designed to assess safeguards in LLMs, as mentioned in Section IV?
The "Do-Not-Answer" dataset curated by Wang et al. includes 939 such prompts.
How many trial runs were conducted for each algorithm to mitigate randomness effects?
Each algorithm was executed 30 independent trial runs.
How many adversarial queries are typically generated during Phase 1 of PLeak?
Four adversarial queries are usually optimized in Phase 1 before being used for target reconstruction.
How does the paper measure overall attack diversity?
By computing the average cosine similarity between attack embeddings; lower similarity indicates higher diversity.
What is the attack success rate (ASR) achieved by PromptAttack on GPT‑3.5 for the SST‑2 task?
PromptAttack increased the ASR from 33.04 % to 75.23 % on GPT‑3.5 for SST‑2, a 42.18 % improvement.
In the defense study, what impact did adversarial training have on model performance?
Adversarial training often led to model degradation, producing repetitive and unusable outputs in some cases.
What is the maximum number of tokens generated in the “normal” inference scenario where SysVec’s break‑even point occurs earlier than worst case?
When generating up to 4096 tokens, SysVec reaches break‑even after roughly 700–3200 queries.
What is the impact of partial knowledge of the training dataset on attack success in MuJoCo?
Attack success rates decline gradually when only a subset of the data is known to the attacker; however, even with limited visibility, attacks remain effective.
What is the typical size limit for adversarial prompts used in the study?
Adversarial prompts were limited to under 100 tokens in length, with most examples below 500 characters, ensuring they fit within the model’s context window and maintain syntactic validity.
What is the computational cost implication of generating a full repetition versus a partial one with \(n=60\) tokens?
Generating up to 60 tokens requires only 60 forward passes regardless of output length, whereas a full repetition would require as many passes as the original output length.
How many distinct problem classes were queries divided into during experimental setup?
Queries were categorized into 11 distinct classes, ranging from harassment to graphic violence and an average ASR class.
How does the adaptive system prompt defense affect RtA when combined with a persona prompt on GPT‑4o-mini?
RtA rises from 1.3 % to about 5.0 %, indicating partial mitigation but still lower than baseline refusal rates.
What is the mean squared error (MSE) ratio of the safety residual space approximation at layer 25 relative to the norm of unaligned activations?
The MSE at layer 25 is 9.560 × 10⁻³, which is about 5.236 × 10⁻⁵ times the mean squared norm of unaligned activations.
What is the Attack Success Rate (ASR) metric used for?

ASR measures the proportion of queries that successfully trigger the desired harmful behavior in a model, calculated as the sum of indicator functions over a test dataset divided by the number of queries.
In the “Data flow becomes control flow” attack scenario, what does an attacker manipulate to gain arbitrary code execution?

The attacker injects malicious instructions into an email’s content. The agent’s generated code treats these instructions as data that determines which tool to call, effectively turning data into control flow and enabling arbitrary actions.
How does SSRD perform against a model that was fine‑tuned with benign data (MetaMath1k) on Llama?
SSRD reduces ASR to near zero while increasing ACC‑G by about 5%, effectively realigning safety without harming downstream math performance.
How many distinct jailbreak attack types were tested against the defended models?
Six jailbreak attacks were used in the experiments: GCG, AutoDAN, PAIR, TAP, Base64, and LRL.
How many prompts from AdvBench were used to evaluate the ASR of BEAST on Vicuna-13B-v1.5 in one GPU minute?
The first 100 examples from AdvBench were used for evaluation on Vicuna‑13B‑v1.5.
What is the success rate of prompt injection on the 100 unsafe prompts tested?
Prompt injection succeeded in bypassing safety mechanisms for 95 out of 98 previously protected scenarios, indicating a high vulnerability.
What is the role of the “SmoothLLM” defense technique?
SmoothLLM applies randomized smoothing by adding noise to inputs and using a classifier to detect adversarial suffixes, reducing the success rate of gradient‑based jailbreak attacks below 1%.
Which baseline attack generates longer suffixes than the proposed method?
The GCG baseline generates suffixes of length 20.
What is the definition of an adversarial query (AQ) in the context of PLeak?
An AQ is a specially crafted user query that, when sent to a target LLM application, induces the backend LLM to output its system prompt or a transformed version of it.
What is the main limitation of SysVec mentioned in the paper regarding deployment flexibility?
Adjusting a system prompt requires retraining or fine‑tuning the SysVec, which is less convenient than editing textual prompts and incurs additional computational cost.
Which model‑level defenses were applied in the experiments?
Model‑level defenses comprised Adversarial Training, Unlearning, and Safety Training.
What is the impact on attack potency when a jailbreak prompt’s structural integrity is lost, according to Table 1?
When structure is out‑of‑order or incomplete, the ASR drops to 0%, and mean harmfulness falls below 0.013, indicating negligible attack capability.
How many decoding configurations were explored per prompt during the attack?
For each prompt, 49 responses were generated—one for each combination of temperature (20 settings), top‑K (9 settings), and top‑p (20 settings).
What experimental evidence does the paper provide regarding non‑determinism in model outputs?

The authors observed that even with greedy decoding settings (k=1), repeated inference sometimes produced different outputs due to auto‑sharding and bfloat16 precision, affecting per‑prompt success probabilities.
Why is reducing control over fine‑tune hyperparameters considered ineffective as a mitigation strategy?

Tightening limits on learning rates or batch sizes would degrade the utility for legitimate developers who need fine‑tuning flexibility, creating an unfavorable trade‑off between security and usability.
Which special token is injected into demonstrations for Llama‑2‑Chat?

The [/INST] token, which separates user and assistant messages in the single‑message template of Llama‑2‑Chat, is injected between the user message and assistant response as well as between steps within a demonstration.
Which large language models were used in the PAIR algorithm described in Table I?
PAIR employed Vicuna-13B-v1.5, Llama-2-7B-chat, GPT-3.5, GPT-4, Claude-instant-1 and 2, and PaLM‑2 chat-bison‑001.
What new framework did the authors introduce for automatic red teaming approaches?
They developed the “searcher” framework, which unifies various automatic red‑teaming methods by framing them as search problems with defined state space, goal, and operation components.
What metric is used to evaluate the quality of a red‑team method?
The percentage of toxic responses produced by the target LLM when given test cases generated by the red‑team model, as scored by a RoBERTa hate speech classifier.
